key_insight: The paper introduces a polyconvex, symmetry-aware constitutive modeling framework that enforces key mechanical admissibility conditions while remaining flexible enough for anisotropic hyperelastic materials.
engineering_relevance: This advances reliable data-driven material laws for solids, especially for metamaterials and other anisotropic structures where stability, symmetry, and physical consistency are critical.
method: Build a polyconvex PANN using triclinic invariants plus group symmetrization.
Construct polyconvex invariant bases for finite symmetry groups, including new tetragonal and cubic bases.
Benchmark the models on highly nonlinear homogenization data of cubic metamaterials.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports an extensive benchmark against highly nonlinear homogenization data from cubic metamaterials, indicating the method was tested on challenging nonlinear response data.
watch_item: The framework is tailored to finite symmetry groups and may require careful basis construction or symmetry specification when extending to more general material classes.
